rules_fired.because: High-viscosity non-protein liquids (e.g., pure glycerol, PEG solutions) resist aspiration, causing back-pressure that stalls the piston and under-delivers volume. Wide-bore tips reduce hydraulic resistance. Reverse pipetting removes the dead-air step. A 2–3 s post-aspirate pause lets the meniscus stabilise before withdrawal.
rules_fired.cite: Eppendorf Technical Guide: Pipetting Viscous Liquids, 2020. Perry's Chemical Engineers' Handbook, 8th ed. (glycerol viscosity 1412 mPa·s at 20°C).

rules_fired.because: Liquids with freeze points at or above 15°C (DMSO ~19°C, glycerol ~18°C) can solidify partially or fully in a 4°C cold room or on ice. Pipetting a partially-solidified liquid causes systematic volume errors. The physical_state guard prevents the rule from misfiring on solids whose melting point happens to be reported in freeze_point_C (e.g. NaOH pellets, PFA powder).
rules_fired.cite: Sigma-Aldrich DMSO Product Information Sheet 276855 (freeze point 18.45°C). Perry's Chemical Engineers' Handbook, 8th ed. (glycerol freeze point 17.8°C).
